# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_selfip (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the self IP to create. If this parameter is not specified, it defaults to the value supplied in the `address` parameter.

### Optional

- `address` (String) The IP addresses for the new self IP. This value is ignored upon update as addresses themselves cannot be changed after they are created. This value is required when creating new self IPs. IPv4 and IPv6 are supported.
- `allow_service` (String) Configure port lockdown for the self IP. By default, the self IP has a "default deny" policy. This can be changed to allow TCP and UDP ports, as well as specific protocols. This list should contain `protocol`:`port` values.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description of the traffic selector.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fw_enforced_policy` (String) Specifies an AFM policy to attach to Self IP.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `netmask` (String) The netmask for the self IP. When creating a new self IP, this value is required.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to manage resources on. You can set different partitions for self IPs, but the address used may not match any other address used by a self IP. Thus, self IPs are not isolated by partitions as other resources on a BIG-IP are.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `route_domain` (Number) The route domain id of the system. When creating a new self IP, if this value is not specified, the default value is `0`. This value cannot be changed after it is set.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `present`, guarantees the self IP exists with the provided attributes. When `absent`, removes the self IP from the system.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `traffic_group` (String) The traffic group for the self IP addresses in an active-active, redundant load balancer configuration. When creating a new self IP, if this value is not specified, the default is `/Common/traffic-group-local-only`.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vlan` (String) The VLAN for the new self IPs. When creating a new self IP, this value is required.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
